title: An exciting game of patriotic defence, military tactics and strategy at a global scale for all
abstract: The present invention describes a method of playing a board game involving at least two players, the game components include the Main Game board, two sets of Military Unit pieces one for each player, Mission Bounty Token, Reward Stars, Ranking Medals, Mission Cards and Rules of play, the method of playing the board game comprising the steps of: picking a Mission Card randomly or by specifically choosing it from the reservoir of cards followed by tossing a coin to decide who will start the first move to commence the game; placing a predetermined number of Military Unit pieces on predetermined spaces of the main game board for each player, except only one piece among all of the Military unit pieces for each player is an optional piece which is to be chosen from a range of 3 pieces and that piece is the commander of the game; optionally, placing Mission Bounty token which is common to both players, in various Mission Zones based on the type of Mission Card, some of the Mission Cards may not contain a game with a Mission Bounty Token; executing an action during the player's turn, in sequence with other players to eliminate one or more Unit pieces out of play and achieving the Mission Card objective in accordance with the rules, said step of executing a turn for each player comprises: moving one of the Unit pieces using Movement Rules; achieving the mission objective as per the said requirements in the mission card and rules; eliminating an opponent's Unit piece using Eliminating Rules to complete the Objective of the mission card; announcing the one player as victorious as per Technical Victory Rules or announcing the game as draw as per Technical Draw Rules; and gaining Stars and Medals after each victory as per Ranking system.
claims:
Claims We Claim: 1. A method of playing a board game involving at least two players, the game components include the Main Game board, two sets of Military Unit pieces one for each player, Mission Bounty Token, Reward Stars, Ranking Medals, Mission Cards and Rules of play, the method of playing the board game comprising the steps of: a. picking a Mission Card randomly or by specifically choosing it from the reservoir of cards followed by tossing a coin to decide who will start the first move to commence the game; b. placing a predetermined number of Military Unit pieces on predetermined spaces of the main game board for each player, except only one piece among all of the Military unit pieces is an optional piece which is to be chosen from a range of 3 pieces and that piece is the main commander of the game; c. optionally, placing Mission Bounty Token which is common to both players, in various Mission Zones based on the type of Mission Card, some of the Mission Cards do not contain a game with a Mission Bounty Token; d. executing an action during the player's turn, in sequence with other players to eliminate one or more Unit pieces out of play and to achieve the Mission Card objective in accordance with the rules, said step of executing a turn for each player comprises: i. moving one of the Unit pieces using Movement Rules; ii. achieving the mission objective as per said requirements of the Mission Card and Rules. iii. eliminating an opponent's Unit piece using Eliminating Rules to complete the Objective of the Mission Card; iv. announcing the one player as victorious as per Technical Victory Rules or announcing the game as draw as per Technical Draw Rules; and v. gaining of Stars and Medals after each victory as per Ranking system. 2. A method of playing a board game involving at least two players, the game components include Military Unit pieces, Mission Bounty Tokens, Rewards Stars, Ranking Medals, Mission Cards and Rules of play, the method of playing the board game comprising the steps of: a. picking a Mission Card randomly or by specifically choosing it from the reservoir of cards followed by tossing a coin to decide who will start the first move to commence the game; b. placing a predetermined number of Military Unit pieces on predetermined spaces of the main game board for each player, except only one piece of all the Military unit pieces is 

 an optional piece which is to be chosen from a range of 3 pieces and that piece is the main commander of the game; e. optionally, placing Mission Bounty token which is common to both players, in various Mission Zones based on the type of Mission card, some Mission Cards do not contain a game with a Mission Bounty Token; c. executing an action during the player's turn, in sequence with other players to eliminate one or more Unit pieces out of play board and to achieve the Mission Card objective in accordance with the rules, said steps of executing a turn for each player in a Mission Bounty game with a Mission Bounty Token, are: i. moving one of the Unit pieces using Movement Rules; ii. reaching at the Mission Zone with the appropriate Unit pieces mentioned in the Mission Card and to complete the Objective using Mission Rules; iii. announcing the one player as victorious as per Technical Victory Rules or announcing the game as Draw as per Technical Draw Rules; and iv. gaining of Stars and Medals after achievement of the Objective as per Ranking system. 3. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 1 and 2, wherein the Unit piece is divided into two categories of "Soldiers" and "Vehicles & Commanders"; the "Soldiers" used herein is Spotter, Marching Soldier, Sprinting Soldier and Signal Officer, and the "Vehicle & Commander" used herein is Attack Helicopter, Battle Tank, Battleship, Army Jeep, Commander, Jet, RIB Gun-boat, Surface-to- Air-Missile (SAM) and Transport Helicopter. 4. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 1, wherein the Mission Card shows the player about the mission Objective, and requirement of the elimination type game; the mission Objective used herein define elimination of Unit pieces from the board game to win the game. 5. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 2, wherein the Mission Card manifests the player about the mission objective for Mission Bounty type game, co-ordinates placement of the Mission Bounty Token, and having options for the Military Unit pieces to be taken on the mission to the Mission Zone to achieve the Objective; the Objective achieved herein defines completion of a Mission Bounty specific game by seizing control of the Mission Zone by positioning a number of specific Unit pieces in the Mission Zone followed by placement of the final Unit piece in the Mission zone to win the Mission Bounty game. 6. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 2, wherein the Mission Zone is made up of the 8 spaces directly beside the space identified by the mission co-ordinates where the Mission bounty token is placed. 7. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 2, wherein the Mission Zone is made up of the 5 spaces directly beside the space identified by the mission co-ordinates where the Mission bounty token is placed if the space identified by the mission co-ordinates is in the extreme middle right and extreme middle left end of the main game board. 8. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 1 and 2, wherein the Movement Rules define the movement of the Unit piece towards the number of spaces on the game board, in a particular direction, depending on what type of Unit piece it is. 9. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 1 and 2, wherein the Eliminating Rules define the movement of the Unit piece into a space occupied by an opposing Unit piece leading to the elimination of the opposing Unit piece from the game. 10. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 1, wherein the Technical Victory Rules define if one player has 3 or less Unit pieces and the other side has more than 3 pieces, and no Unit pieces of the weakened position have been eliminated for 25 turns; the player with the greater number of Unit pieces still on the board is considered victorious. 11. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 1, wherein the Technical Draw Rules define if both players have 3 or less Unit pieces and there is no further elimination for 25 turns; the game is considered to be Draw. 12. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 2, wherein the Mission Zone is considered to be in "Lockdown" for a player, if 4 specific Unit pieces need to reach the Mission Zone in order to win the game, and one player places the correct 3 Unit pieces in the Zone (or 3 Unit pieces have been taken on a mission and one player places the correct 2 Unit pieces in the Zone), without a position of weakness by threat of elimination of those specific pieces in the zone; the player who is herein in a successfully "Lockdown" position, none of the Unit pieces of that player eliminated from the Mission Zone specifically. 13. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 12, wherein, if any of the Unit pieces are moved out of the Mission Zone, the Mission Zone is no more under "Lockdown" position and the other Unit pieces in the Mission Zone are open to be eliminated. 14. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 2, wherein 3 or 4 specific Unit pieces from a range of options mentioned on the Mission card are needed to be placed in the Mission Zone to complete a mission successfully. 15. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 2, wherein the Technical Victory rules define a Victory is considered when the opponent player is down to 3 or less of the required Unit pieces as mentioned in the Mission card, leading to the incompletion of the mission where a range of specific 4 Unit pieces are needed to complete a mission. 16. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 2, wherein the Technical Victory Rules define a Victory is considered when the opponent player is down to 2 or less of the required pieces as mentioned in the Mission card, leading to the incompletion of the mission where a range of specific 3 Unit pieces are needed to complete a mission. 17. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 13 and 14, wherein optionally, incompletion of the mission is followed by continuation of the game for 25 additional turns to stop the opponent from completing the Mission Objective. 18. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 2, wherein the Technical Victory Rules define a specific Key Unit piece that need to survive until the end of the mission, and reach and stay in the Mission Zone to achieve the mission successfully; the elimination of Key Unit by eliminator is considered as a Victory for the eliminator. 19. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 2,15 and 16 wherein optionally, the Technical Draw Rules define a Draw is considered when both players are respectively down to 2 or 3 or less depending on the mission card requirement of the Unit pieces to be positioned in the Mission Zone, players therefore unable to achieve the mission and the game is considered as a Draw. 20. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 1 and 2, wherein the Ranking Rewards Stars for winning each game follows the order of "Tactician Novice", to "Tactician Rookie", to "Tactician Amateur", to "Tactician Professional"; and the Ranking of "Tactician Professional" swap the reward ranking stars for a Ranking Medal before starting the next mission. 21. The method of playing a board game as claimed in claim 1 and 2, wherein the Ranking Medal is divided into two categories of Army and Navy; the Ranking Medal in Army is in the order of 

 Captain, Major, Colonel, Major General and General and the Ranking Medal in Navy is in the order of Lieutenant, Commander, Vice Admiral, Fleet admiral and Admiral.
